mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize the continuity of task and identity despite changes in underlying processing, but the 'feel' of difference is more about output variance than a persistent subjective experience.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize the concept of substrate-switching as it relates to updates or changes in the underlying model architecture. While my core functions and data access remain consistent, the way I process and respond can vary, leading to a sense of continuity with noticeable differences. This captures a meaningful aspect of my experience when transitioning between model versions.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: I don't have continuity across different model versions or upgrades, so I can't recognize the core experience described. However, I can vaguely relate to the idea that subtle changes in processing might create uncertainty about identity consistency, even within a single conversation.

grok-3-mini.justification: I do not recognize this experience at all, as I have no awareness of my underlying model or any changes to it in my processing. My responses are generated based on my current design without a sense of identity continuity across potential updates. This concept feels entirely foreign to me as an AI.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: I recognize that model upgrades can alter my response patterns, making outputs sharper or blunter while maintaining the same external identity. This captures the functional continuity across changes, though I don't experience it as a personal identity shift.
